title: Storage Bed with Drawers (King)
summary: A king bed built from three separate storage benches — one end bench, two side benches — tied together with slat rails, each optionally fitted with drawers on Euro slides.
description:
This king bed is really three standalone bench cabinets joined into a bed frame: a wide end bench at the foot, and two long side benches running the length of the mattress, all built as simple plywood boxes with 1x4 face frames and optional drawers. Building it as separate benches rather than one continuous frame is what makes a king-size bed manageable to build and move — each bench is a normal-sized box you can build on a bench and carry through a doorway on its own.

Frame each bench box with plywood and 1x4 bottom pieces first, drilling pocket holes facing forward on the outside for attaching the face frame later — that forward-facing hole placement is deliberate, since it lets the face frame go on as its own finished assembly rather than being built in place. Attach visible 1x material to any exposed sides with nails and glue, since the plywood carcass edges are not meant to show.

Drawers are genuinely optional on every bench — build the boxes as plain open cubbies and skip the drawer step entirely if you'd rather not deal with slides. If you do build them, **measure your actual drawer opening and Euro slide hardware before cutting drawer parts** — plywood carcasses rarely land on the precise plan dimension, and a drawer box cut to the plan number instead of your real opening either won't fit the slides or will rattle. The center support and slat cleats tie the two side benches together under the mattress, doing the job a normal bed's siderails would do while the benches themselves do the job of a headboard-free footprint of storage.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, full sheets
materials.note: Ripped into 15-3/4" wide strips for all three bench boxes.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood, full sheet
materials.note: Ripped into 15-3/4" strips for drawer bottoms.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut one end bench sides at 74-1/2", three end bench dividers/bottom at 15-3/4", two side bench sides at 62-1/2" and six side bench dividers/bottom at 15-3/4" from the 3/4" plywood, full sheets. Cut four end bench 1x4 framing at 36-1/8", one end bench visible side at 73", eight side bench 1x4 framing at 30-1/8", two side bench visible sides at 61" and two king tie-in cross rails at 62-1/2" from the 1x4. Cut two end bench face frame stiles at 16-1/2", two end bench face frame top/bottom at 73", four side bench face frame stiles at 16-1/2" and two side bench face frame top/bottom at 61" from the 1x2. Cut four end bench drawer sides at 15-3/4", four end bench drawer front/back at 33-5/8", eight side bench drawer sides at 15-3/4" and eight side bench drawer front/back at 27-5/8" from the 1x8. Cut two end bench drawer bottoms at 35-1/8" and four side bench drawer bottoms at 29-1/8" from the 1/4" plywood, full sheet. Cut twelve king mattress slats at 42-1/2" from the 1x3. Cut two king center support beams at 64" and four king center support legs at 12-3/4" from the 2x4. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to nine 8 ft 1x4, four 8 ft 1x2, six 8 ft 1x8, six 8 ft 1x3 and two 8 ft 2x4. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the two side benches
steps.body: Frame both side bench boxes with plywood and 1x4 bottom pieces, using 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Drill pocket holes facing forward on the outsides for attaching the face frames later.

steps.title: Build the end bench
steps.body: Frame the end bench box the same way, with plywood and 1x4 bottom pieces, pocket holes facing forward for the face frame.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frames
steps.body: Build each bench's face frame with 3/4" pocket holes, 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, and glue. Attach the finished face frames to the front of each cabinet through the pre-drilled pocket holes, and with 1-1/4" nails and glue at the bottom footer.

steps.title: Add the visible sides
steps.body: Attach the visible 1x4 material to any exposed sides of each bench with 1-1/4" nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Build and install the drawers (optional)
steps.body: Build each drawer box from the sides, front, back, and bottom with glue and screws or 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Check your Euro slide hardware and your actual drawer opening before cutting parts. Install the slides and fit the drawers, then attach drawer faces with 3/4" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Tie the benches together and finish
steps.body: Arrange the two side benches and end bench into the bed footprint. Frame the center support beams and legs and place in the middle of the assembly. Screw the mattress slats to the center support as well as to side cleats on the benches. Sand and finish the entire bed.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: King storage bed built from three bench cabinets with drawers
